mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces corporate borrowing costs and compresses equity discount rates, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.5 percent, labor market slack suppresses wage growth and consumer spending, triggering negative corporate earnings revisions and S&P 500 declines over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 55 percent of triggered months, or the signal loses predictive power during recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts signal a synchronized contraction in consumer demand, causing immediate negative repricing of cyclical equities.
failure_condition: Joint decline periods show positive alpha greater than 0.5 percent monthly, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, a month-over-month drop in industrial production indicates supply-side stress that triggers immediate risk-off flows and negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Short-term returns during tightening plus supply drops are not significantly negative with a p-value greater than 0.1 in a two-tailed t-test.

mechanism_narrative: Rising policy rates combined with falling unemployment create stagflationary pressure that forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy longer, depressing equity valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns exceed plus 1 percent in more than 60 percent of triggered quarters, invalidating the stagflation risk premium channel.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth falling below 1.5 percent signals economic stagnation that triggers immediate downward revisions in S&P 500 earnings forecasts, producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Twenty-day returns following sub-1.5 percent GDP prints average above plus 0.2 percent, or the signal fails to predict direction in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly payroll additions directly increase aggregate income and consumer spending capacity, generating immediate positive momentum in broad equity indices over the following week.
failure_condition: Five-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with payroll surprises, with a Spearman rho below 0.05 over a rolling three-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production alongside accelerating inflation indicates demand-pull price pressures that erode real corporate margins, leading to negative equity returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Margin compression does not materialize and forward returns are positive in more than 50 percent of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant.
